paper_title: Digital Terrain Model Analysis of Fenland Basin

methods.method_name: Digital Terrain Model Construction and Analysis
methods.description: This method involves constructing a digital terrain model (DTM) to interpret the Fenland 'islands' and associated ridges as marginal push-moraine complexes. The DTM helps in understanding the glacial origin and the landscape forms indicating the advance of the Tottenhill ice lobe.

steps.description: Construct a digital terrain model (DTM) of the basin using a 50 m cell size.
steps.searchable_summary: construct DTM with 50 m cell size

steps.description: Generate a hillshade from the DTM at an azimuth of 045° and an altitude of 45°.
Interpret the landforms from the DTM and hillshade to demonstrate the alignment of ridge-like hills in a lobate form.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret landforms from DTM and hillshade

steps.description: Compare the form and distribution of the ridges with other similar lobate glacial basins to confirm the glacial origin.
steps.searchable_summary: compare ridge forms with other glacial basins
